subject: Biology
title: Ribosomes: Protein Factories
description: A micro-lesson that makes Ribosomes: Protein Factories usable.

hook.text: Ribosomes read messenger RNA and stitch amino acids into proteins, one by one.

buildup.text: A single cell can contain millions of ribosomes, all building proteins at once.

discovery.text: They're made of RNA and protein, assembled in the nucleolus, then shipped out to work.

twist.text: Antibiotics like erythromycin jam bacterial ribosomes without harming yours.

climax.text: Every enzyme, hormone, and structural fiber in your body was built by a ribosome.

punchline.text: Tiny machines that build everything alive.

quiz.question: How do some antibiotics fight bacteria?
